author: s1na
content:
It is possible in geth to import history from EraE files yes. If I were to give an overview of history expiry status within Geth.

**What we have**
*(High priority):*
- Exporting EraE files from full nodes for any range of history
- Importing EraE files to bootstrap a node with history, and then do a state sync on it
*(Lower prio):*
- Pruning up to merge and prague which is roughly 1 year history.
- Dropping in files to a pruned node so it can access history if they need
- Ability to sync a node without downloading pre-merge and pre-prague history

**What we still need to implement**
*(High priority):*
- Hardening p2p downloader to grafeully handle nodes not having history
*(Low priority):*
- Rolling expiry and export (only after we have done the previous item)

author: siladu
content:
> Pruning up to merge and prague which is roughly 1 year history.
Was there a cross-client discussion about this somewhere I missed? 
If as a network/community, we are comfortable with this, then I wonder if people are also already comfortable with rolling history?
In Besu for example, we could implement rolling history easier than a pre-prague history drop. 
I haven't looked into the p2p implications closely though.

Does this https://data.ethpandaops.io/erae/mainnet/ cover full history? How often is it updated?

author: s1na
content:
> Was there a cross-client discussion about this somewhere I missed? 
I think pruning has already been explored across EL clients in different forms, including rolling-style approaches in some cases. Geth, and I believe Besu as well, have stayed more on the conservative side so far

> In Besu for example, we could implement rolling history easier than a pre-prague history drop. 
Curious how that is? Is it because you don't have the pre-merge pruning yet?

> Does this https://data.ethpandaops.io/erae/mainnet/ cover full history? How often is it updated?
It covers history until a month ago or so. For now if it was ever too behind you can ping me, but plan is to have it updated on a batch-by-batch basis

author: s1na
content: On the coordination point, I don’t think we’ve had a strong cross-client discussion on pruning strategy yet. Would be good to align on pre-prague drop vs rolling

author: siladu
content:
Besu has had rolling ability for a long time for non-mainnet use cases, but might need to tweak for mainnet e.g. flag UX.

We coded pre-merge pruning features quite explicitly so would need to update a few things to make pre-prague nice for production, though the core of the change (syncing from prague block) would just be updating a checkpoint block in the genesis file https://github.com/besu-eth/besu/blob/b2cbdeae018db7b6bf362b1bbf01a1d512e00d01/config/src/main/resources/mainnet.json#L78-L81

Either way is quite trivial, we were just waiting for client agreement really. If rolling is good to go, then would rather go straight to that.

author: s1na
content:
Makes sense, that’s helpful context.

I agree both approaches seem quite doable. Would be good to get input from other clients here as well.

author: s1na
content: In the meantime, having EraE support across clients feels pretty important

author: milos88
content: As of now, there is cross-client agreement on prunning pre-merge history (except headers). Not sure if you implied/assumed this or not.

author: s1na
content: Right The agreement was to be prepared for pre-merge pruning by last May I believe. Looking forward, I meant there is no cross-client agreement on what is the next step for pruning, i.e. whether it should be pre-pectra or go straight to rolling expiry.

author: funnygiulio
content: you can even just say to prune arbitrairly from block 24mn onwards to make it easier and then as time go on, we just agree on stronger pruning

author: s1na
content: The difference of minimum history and pre-pectra is around 60Gbs on geth, just adding for context. We get most of the gain already on mainnet. For newer networks (testnets etc) rolling expiry clear win

author: s1na
content: Another angle to look at is if a client supports L2s they'd have to configure each separately whereas "keep last 100k blocks" is universal

author: funnygiulio
content: you can even just say keep last 100k blocks and then people can keep more if they want to

author: funnygiulio
content: if we want to keep last 1mn by default we definetely can, with the expectation others wont

author: funnygiulio
content: You can prune more with minimal config

author: s1na
content:
It feels like the main question to align on is whether we want to standardize around:
-    a bounded cutoff (e.g. pre-pectra), or
-    a rolling window

Maybe it makes sense to gather input from all EL clients and then sync on this explicitly on ACD
